compliance_framework: NCAA CARA (Collegiate Athlete Responsibilities and Accountability)

practice_hours.description: Maximum allowable practice/training per calendar day

weekly_hours.description: Maximum allowable practice/training per academic week (Sunday-Saturday)

training_sessions.description: No more than 2 organized practice sessions per day with minimum 4-hour rest

mandatory_rest.description: At least 1 complete rest day per week; minimum 4 consecutive days off per calendar month

off_season_restrictions.description: Off-season (outside competition dates) limited to strength/conditioning only
off_season_restrictions.note: Definitions of on-season vs off-season vary by sport

what_does_not_count: Voluntary individual training (gym workouts without coach)
Casual workouts not organized by coaching staff
gray_areas: Film review (counts if mandatory, not if voluntary)
Team meals (counts only if structured activity time)

championship_tournament_week.duration: Maximum 7 consecutive days per calendar year
championship_tournament_week.notes: NCAA allows temporary increase during official championship tournament weeks

monitoring_requirements.tracking_method: Coach must log all practice hours daily
monitoring_requirements.documentation: Coach signature required on all logs

second_violation.consequence: Reduced allowed hours for 30 days, possible partial eligibility suspension

third_violation.consequence: Full eligibility suspension until compliance certification completed

work_iq_thresholds.note: ScoutFlow AI should flag alerts when approaching 87.5% of daily and 90% of weekly limits

additional_restrictions: All practices must be scheduled 48 hours in advance with compliance office
school_specific_rules.approval_authority: Director of Athletics, Compliance Officer: compliance@stateuniversity.edu
